stemHtml: What are the fences on top of the wing used for?

options.a: To prevent spanwise airflow along the wing, which could otherwise lead to tip stalling.
options.b: To produce a spanwise flow of high energy air, in order to reduce the amount of chordwise airflow on the wing.
options.c: To generate a vortex from the engine nacelle, acting as a chordwise fence, reducing the spanwise airflow of the wing.
options.d: To produce strong vortices over the lower surface of the wing, in order to keep its boundary layer attached.
